firm.name: Zerbach & Company
firm.oneLiner: Unabhängige, international tätige M&A-Beratung mit Fokus auf den Mittelstand, für Unternehmensverkauf, Nachfolge, Zukauf, Bewertung, Debt Advisory und Corporate Finance.
fitNotes: Clear fit for an AI-native M&A platform because they run structured sell-side and buy-side processes and explicitly mention buyer identification, outreach, and due diligence preparation.
They emphasize a senior-led process from first meeting to closing, which suggests value in workflow automation that preserves control and confidentiality.
They already use large buyer databases and international reach, so tools that improve search, prioritization, and process visibility could be relevant.
No client portal or data room is stated on the site, so the strongest angle is process support rather than replacing an existing platform.

likelyPriorities: Inference: would care about preserving a senior-led process and client trust.
Inference: likely values tools that help manage buyer outreach and transaction workflow without adding delegation risk.

Inference: likely interested in better process control, visibility, and documentation.

signals.detail: Keynote at the Financial Planner Tage 2026 on company valuation.

signals.title: Published studies and newsletters
signals.detail: Site lists studies and industry newsletters such as Packaging 2020, Elektrotechnik 2016, and Umformtechnik 2014.

signals.title: Founded in 2014
signals.detail: Firm says it was founded in 2014 on Gut Maarhausen in Cologne.

play.angle: Lead with the fact that Zerbach & Company says it was founded in 2014 on Gut Maarhausen in Cologne, and use that to frame the note around a firm that has built a long-running, senior-led process. Given their emphasis on confidentiality and anonymized outreach, avoid sounding like a generic software pitch or pushing a client portal first. Instead, position Fintalo as a way to keep buyer management, NDA flow, and document release controlled without adding more email and spreadsheet overhead.

objections.objection: Wir arbeiten bewusst diskret und senior-led, so etwas darf den Prozess nicht verkomplizieren.
objections.counter: Absolutely, that is the point of Fintalo, it keeps the workflow controlled in one place and reduces handoffs rather than adding another layer of process.

objections.objection: Wir haben bereits funktionierende Abläufe mit E-Mail und Excel.
objections.counter: That is common, and Fintalo is meant to replace the sprawl only where it creates friction, especially around NDA handling, buyer tracking, and staged document sharing.

objections.objection: Unsere Mandate sind unterschiedlich, eine Standardlösung passt vermutlich nicht.
objections.counter: Understood, the platform is flexible enough to support sell-side, buy-side, and debt advisory workflows, so the demo can focus on the parts that matter to your team.

openingEmail.subject: A simple way to run more of the deal process in one place
openingEmail.body:
Hi Leander,

I noticed Zerbach & Company was founded in 2014 on Gut Maarhausen in Cologne. Firms that have grown with a senior-led, confidential process often end up carrying a lot of deal work across email and Excel, especially once buyer outreach and document release start to scale.

Fintalo is built to keep NDA workflows, buyer management, and stage-gated document sharing in one place, without changing how a partner wants to run the process.

Would you be open to a short demo next week?

Best,
openingEmail.groundedFact: Zerbach & Company says it was founded in 2014 on Gut Maarhausen in Cologne.
